descriptions.text: A very big wall built across a river. It holds back the water so a long lake forms behind it. When some water is let through, it falls a long way down and turns big wheels, and the turning wheels make power for the homes and lights of a city.

A tall box in your home that keeps food cold so it stays good to eat for many days. It works by moving heat from the inside of the box to the outside. There is a small light inside that turns on when you open the door.

Stairs that move by themselves. You stand still on one step and it carries you up or down to the next floor, so you do not have to walk at all. At the top, the steps turn flat and slide back down under the floor to start over again.

A set of soft little roads inside your body. A strong working part in the middle of you pushes red life water through these roads all day and all night, without ever stopping, to bring air and food to every small piece of you and to carry the bad stuff away.

Many computers all over the world joined together so they can talk to each other. When you ask for a page, small pieces of it travel through lines under the ground and through the air from far away, and they are put back together in front of you in less than a second.

A very big building where they break the smallest pieces of stuff in the world to make heat. The heat turns water into hot air that pushes big wheels around very fast, and the turning wheels make the lights come on in many far away homes. The people who run it must be very careful, because the stuff inside can make people very sick if it gets out.

A high hill with a deep hole at the top that goes far down into the ground. Sometimes very hot soft rock from deep under the ground comes up through the hole with fire and dark air, and runs down the sides. New ground is made where it cools.

A small box in your home that makes food hot very fast. It sends waves you can not see into the food. The waves make the little bits of water inside the food move very fast, and all that moving is what makes the food hot. It often has a table inside that turns.

The way green living things make their own food. They take light from the sun, water from the ground, and part of the air, and turn them into food so they can grow. While they do this, they give off the very part of the air that we need to stay alive.

A long boat with a hard skin that can go all the way under the water and stay down there for many days. The people inside breathe air that the boat makes for them. It can hide deep in the dark water where no one can see it.

A doctor puts a tiny bit of a very weak or dead form of a bad thing into your arm. Your body learns how to fight it without getting very sick. Later, if the real bad thing tries to get in, your body remembers it and wins the fight fast.

A small flat hard thing you carry with you that lets you buy things now and pay for them later. When you use it, the money house pays for you right away. At the end of the month you must pay the money house back, and if you are late, you will owe them even more.

A little room that moves up and down inside a tall building. You step in, press the number of the floor you want, and it carries you there, pulled by strong lines from above, so you never have to walk up all those stairs.

A very big bag full of hot air, with a small open box hanging under it for people to stand in. A fire makes the air in the bag hot, and because hot air always wants to go up, the whole thing rises off the ground and rides the wind across the sky.

A very bright line of light that jumps between a dark sky and the ground when the weather is bad. It is much hotter than the sun for a tiny moment, and a great loud sound comes after it. It likes to hit high things that stand alone.

A box in your home that cleans your clothes for you. You put the clothes in with water and cleaning stuff, close the door, and it turns them around and around until the dirt comes out. At the end it spins very fast so they come out only a little wet.

You type a few words about what you want to know, and in less than a second it hands you a long list of pages from all over the world that talk about it, with the best ones first. It has already read almost everything, so you do not have to.

A very strong little box that rides in the tail of a flying machine. It remembers everything the machine does and every word said up front. It is built to live through fire and water and a very hard hit, so that if something goes wrong, people can find it and learn what happened.
It looks like a quiet high hill, but it is really a door to the deep hot inside of the world. When it wakes up, it throws hot rock and fire high into the sky, and everyone who lives near it must run.
Without this box, your food would turn bad in a day or two. It is the coldest place in your home, and people often stand in front of it with the door open, looking inside for something good to eat.

It is not in any one place, but it is everywhere. It lets you see faces from the other side of the world, read almost any book, watch anything, buy anything, and say anything, without ever leaving your chair.

Before this was made safe, buildings could not be very tall, because no one wants to walk up forty floors. It is a little room on strong lines that goes up and down all day, and people stand in it quietly, watching the numbers change.

A place with tall round parts that let out white clouds of warm wet air. Deep inside, the smallest bits of a heavy stuff are split, and the huge heat that comes out is turned into the force that lights up a whole city.

People build a great thick wall right where a river runs through a narrow gap in the hills. The water backs up into a huge still body behind it, and as it is let out down below, the push of its fall is turned into power.

Think of your body as a land full of tiny red streams. In the center sits a strong part that never rests, sending the red stuff out to feed every far piece of you and then pulling it all the way back to start again.

A cold box that stands softly in the corner of the room where food is kept. By pulling the heat out of the air inside, it keeps your milk and greens good for far longer than they would last on a warm shelf.

Almost every computer on earth is tied together into one huge thing. Through it, a note or a picture can jump from one side of the world to the other in less time than it takes to shut your eyes, using lines and air.

For years it may sleep like a normal hill. Then one day the ground shakes, and from the top comes hot melted rock, dark dust, and fire, running down the sides in bright lines that burn all they touch.
A little box that heats your food in under a minute. It fills the food with waves you can not see, which shake the water in it so fast that the whole plate turns hot while the box itself stays cool.

A set of steps that carry you along on their own. You just step on and stand, and a hidden part below pulls the whole line of steps up to the floor above, then folds them flat and takes them back down.

Green leaves are like tiny kitchens run on light. They drink up water from below and one part of the air from above, and with the help of the sun they cook it all into food, letting out clean air for us to breathe.

A ship built to live below the waves. It fills parts of itself with water to go down and pushes the water back out to come up, and the people shut inside can stay down in the dark for weeks with the air it makes.

A clever way to teach your body to fight before it ever gets sick. A doctor gives you a tiny taste of a weak dead thing, so that if the real one shows up later, your body already knows just how to win.

A thin flat slip of hard stuff in your bag that stands in for money. You wave or slide it to pay, and a big money house covers the cost for now, trusting that you will pay it all back when the month is done.
A small moving room that saves your legs in a tall building. You walk in, press a number, and strong lines pull you up through the middle of the building to the floor you want.

A great cloth ball that carries people up into the open air. A hot fire heats the air held in the ball, and since warm air likes to climb, the whole thing lifts off the ground and rides wherever the wind blows.

On a dark and wild night, the sky and the ground build up too much of the same push, until it all lets go at once in a blinding white line. The air it tears through gets so hot it makes a great crack of sound.

A round door on a box in the corner where your dirty clothes go in. It spins them round and round in warm water and soap to lift the dirt away, then turns very fast to throw most of the water back out.

A helper that has read nearly every page in the world so you do not have to. You give it a word or two about what you are after, and in a blink it hands back a long, sorted list of the best places to look.

A tough little case that sits in the back of every big flying machine. It quietly writes down what the machine does and what the people up front say, so that after a bad day, others can read it and learn.
It is like a class for the inside of your body. A doctor puts in a small, safe copy of a bad thing, and your body studies it and learns to beat it, so the real thing can never make you very sick.

This is how green leaves make food out of almost nothing. Using the light of the sun as their power, they take in water and one part of the air, and turn it into the sweet stuff they need to grow taller and stronger.
